one_liner: AI Chief of Staff as a Service -- a managed quad-AI hive (Claude, Gemini, Codex, Perplexity) that autonomously runs small business workflows, logs every action to Slack, and requires zero prompt engineering from the client.
problem: Solopreneurs and small agencies spend 2-4 hours daily on repeatable, low-decision work (drafting replies, generating reports, running outreach). They know AI can help but cannot get past the setup complexity, fragmentation of tools, and lack of a reliable audit trail.
solution: A multi-tenant SaaS dashboard where clients connect their API keys once, pick from pre-built session templates, and let the hive dispatch the right AI model for each subtask automatically. All agent actions are logged to Slack in real time for human oversight.
icp: Solo business owners and 2-5 person agencies with $80k-$1.5M ARR, already paying for at least one AI subscription (ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro), comfortable with SaaS tools but not developers, spending $100-$200/mo on tools that demonstrably save time.
revenue_model: Subscription SaaS. Spark $49/mo (100 sessions, 1 user, 3 integrations), Hive $129/mo (unlimited sessions, 5 users, 20 integrations), Enterprise $399/mo (unlimited everything, white-label, SLA). Annual billing at 20% discount. Overage at $0.75/session beyond Spark limit.
moat: The combination of (1) multi-model hive routing with model attribution transparency, (2) Slack-native audit logging as a trust mechanism for autonomous AI actions, and (3) the session template library built specifically for SMB workflows -- none of which any direct competitor currently offers as a bundled product.
competitors: Zapier AI (Zapier Agents) -- no multi-model routing, no audit trail
Make.com -- no AI reasoning, pure if/then logic
Relevance AI -- no Slack audit log, enterprise-only positioning
Notion AI -- locked to Notion ecosystem, not autonomous
AutoGPT / AgentGPT -- requires self-hosting, no multi-tenancy, no support
ChatGPT Team -- requires human prompting, not autonomous
mvp_features: F01: Multi-tenant auth and onboarding (Google OAuth + email/password, 3-step onboarding checklist)
F02: Integration Vault -- AES-256 encrypted API key storage for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, Perplexity, Slack
F03: Hive Session Builder -- task type selection, template fill-in, schedule configuration
F04: AI Hive Dispatcher -- model routing by task type, multi-model synthesis, retry + fallback logic
F05: Slack Audit Logger -- structured session event logs, channel selection, graceful degradation
F06: Session Output Viewer -- full output render, model attribution, download (MD/JSON/TXT)
F07: Dashboard Home -- stats, quick launch, recent sessions, integration health panel
F08: Billing and Subscriptions -- Stripe, 7-day free trial, Spark + Hive + Enterprise tiers
F09: Session Templates Library -- 12 pre-built SMB workflow templates
F10: Workspace Settings -- tenant profile, notifications, Slack channel config
F11: Cost Estimation and Usage Tracking -- pre-session cost estimate, monthly AI spend tracking, alert threshold
F12: Webhook Trigger Endpoints -- HMAC-validated inbound triggers per session template
F13: Session History and Replay -- searchable history, re-run, date + status filters
F14: Error Handling and Retry Logic -- exponential backoff, model fallback, clear error states in UI
F15: Email Notification Digest -- daily/weekly digest, session complete + error notifications
excluded_from_mvp: Integrations Marketplace (Gmail, HubSpot, Shopify, Notion, etc.) -- Phase 2
White-label mode (custom domain + branding) -- Phase 2 / Enterprise
Agent Memory Store (cross-session context persistence) -- Phase 2
Client portal mode for agencies -- Phase 2
Public API and developer SDK -- Phase 3

recommended_stack.database: PostgreSQL via Supabase (row-level security for multi-tenancy)
recommended_stack.session_queue: BullMQ backed by Redis (durable, retryable jobs)
recommended_stack.secrets: Supabase Vault or AWS Secrets Manager (API key encryption)
recommended_stack.hosting: Vercel (frontend), Railway or Render (FastAPI backend + worker)

risks: R01 (HIGH): AI provider API instability -- mitigated by multi-model fallback routing and provider-independent platform availability
R02 (HIGH): Tenant API key credential theft -- mitigated by AES-256 encryption, secrets manager separation, credentials never returned via API
R03 (HIGH): LLM output quality variance -- mitigated by review-before-delivery default, regenerate button, user expectation setting in onboarding
R04 (HIGH): Runaway AI costs from compromised keys -- mitigated by per-session cost caps, rate limits, monthly spend alerts, tenant-owns-their-keys architecture
R05 (HIGH): Onboarding drop-off due to product complexity -- mitigated by platform key option, template-first UX, video onboarding, live chat on onboarding screen
R09 (HIGH): Single founder bus factor -- mitigated by managed infrastructure, automated monitoring, documented runbook before launch
